tags](https://github.com/apple/container/tags)\n\n[0.8.0](https://github.com/apple/container/releases/tag/0.8.0)\n\nImportant\n\nThis release addresses a low severity security issue ([CVE-2026-20613](https://github.com/apple/containerization/security/advisories/GHSA-cq3j-qj2h-6rv3)) in the containerization library whereby a poorly assembled or maliciously crafted image tar archive can write files to locations other than the extraction directory. The issue is present when a user runs the&#96;container image load&#96; command (or the &#96;cctl image load command in containerization)\n\nNo privilege escalation is possible by exploiting the issue; the archive extractor can only write files that the user could write themselves.\n\n## Highlights\n\n⌨️ denotes breaking CLI changes.\n\n👩‍💻 denotes breaking API changes.\n\nCore\n\n- [Prevent container image load from writing files outside the extraction directory](https://github.com/apple/container/pull/1056)\n- [Fixed panics filesystem data integrity errors when stressing containers](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/614)\n- Numerous stability fixes for container start and stop.\n- [container system version shows version info](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/824)\n- [&#96;--read-only command for container create/run mounts root filesystem read-only](https://github.com/apple/container/pull/999)\n- [Add platform architecture aliases for amd64 and arm64](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/1035)\n- 👩‍💻 [Reorganized client APIs](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/461) and numerous other API changes\n\nNetwork\n\n- [container network prune removes unused networks](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/893)\n- [IPv6 network configuration, container DNS, and port forwarding](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/460)\n\nStorage\n\n- [Improve volume filesystem performance](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/882)\n- [Translate block mount options correctly, use sync mode by default](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/876)\n\n## What's Changed\n\n- fix(TerminalProgress): make the progress bar respect locale-specific decimal separator by [#416](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/416) changes the location of the image store for&#96;container&#96; from&#96;~/Library/Application\\ Support/com.apple.container/.build&#96; to&#96;~/Library/Application\\ Support/com.apple.container/builder&#96;. If you want to retain the content of your old store, stop&#96;container&#96;, move the old directory to the new pathname (replacing the&#96;.build&#96; directory if necessary), and start the application again. Or, you can delete the unused&#96;.build&#96; directory when you want to recover the storage capacity.\n\n⌨️ denotes breaking CLI changes.\n\n👩‍💻 denotes breaking API changes.\n\nCore\n\n- [Rename .build directory to builder in application data](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/416)\n- [Recognize multiple build --tag options](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/732)\n- [Correct behavior in env files](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/691)\n\nNetwork\n\n- [Add network create --subnet](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/458)\n- [Add container run --network none for containers with no network connectivity](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/386)\n- [Correct behavior for proxy environment variables for image fetch](https://github.com/apple/containerization/issues/255)\n- [Correct behavior for proxy environment variables for kernel fetch](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/466)\n\nStorage\n\n- [Add anonymous volumes](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/726)\n- [Implicit creation of named volumes](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/690)\n- [Add volume changes.\n\nCore\n\n- ⌨️ [Remove images alias for container image.](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/596)\n- ⌨️ [container system property commands for managing settings.](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/384)\n- ⌨️ [Use com.apple.container.registry as keychain ID.](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/644). You will need to perform&#96;container registry login&#96; again for your registries to establish new keychain entries. You can delete the old&#96;com.apple.container&#96; keychain entries using the Keychain Access tool.\n- 👩‍💻 [Expose ContainerCommands for external use.](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/609)\n- 👩‍💻 [Use APIServer for sandbox management operations.](https://github.com/apple/container/pull/628). This PR includes significant changes to the ContainerClient API.\n- [container image save can save multiple images.](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/558)\n- [Revert inadvertent pull platform filtering.](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/592) Note that this will cause image pull times to be longer for now, as observed in the [original issue](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/83).\n- [Cleaned up help output for commands.](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/515)\n\nNetwork\n\n- [Add --labels for networks.](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/557)\n\nStorage\n\n- [Fixed regression on relative path as a virtiofs mount source.](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/565)\n\nFull tags](https://github.com/apple/container/tags)\n\n[0.4.1](https://github.com/apple/container/releases/tag/0.4.1)\n\nMore fixes and features in the August release...thank you for all of the contributions!\n\nThe most notable feature in this release are named volumes (&#96;container volume create&#96;). One difference between named volumes in&#96;container&#96; and in other systems is that you can't read-write a named volume across multiple running containers (VMs).\n\nWhy no 0.4.0? We had to discard that tag due to a couple test regressions.\n\n## Highlights\n\nCore\n\n- [systemd can run in containers](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/92)\n- [Unified --platform, --os, --arch flag support](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/231)\n- [Improved interactive input handling (-i flag)](https://github.com/apple/container/pull/483)\n- [Consistent --version output](https://github.com/apple/container/pull/517)\n\nNetwork\n\n- [Automatic /etc/hosts generation](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/314)\n- [DNS hostname conflict prevention](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/394)\n- [Prevent deletion of referenced networks](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/392)\n- [SSH auth socket forwarding for run and exec commands](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/498)\n\nStorage\n\n- [Create and manage named volumes with container volume.](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/339)\n\nPlugins\n\n- [Custom application data root support with --app-root](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/418)\n\n## What's Changed\n\n- Add initial native builder code by tags](https://github.com/apple/container/tags)\n\n[0.3.0](https://github.com/apple/container/releases/tag/0.3.0)\n\nA big thank you to all contributors for your work to improve container!\n\nBeginning with the 0.3.0 release, we're aiming to release on a monthly cycle. For now, each monthly release will increment the minor version. Should we need to create an off cycle patch release, we'll bump the patch version.\n\n## Highlights\n\nCore\n\n- [Enumerate a well known path where user-installed plugins can be found](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/340)\n- [Fix output of plugin details in CLI help](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/349)\n- [Fix handling of process options in container create](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/395)\n- [Enable nested virtualization in containers](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/376)\n- Several fixes for thread safety\n\nNetwork\n\n- ```
OVERVIEW: Set the default kernel

USAGE: container system kernel set [--arch &lt;arch&gt;] [--binary &lt;binary&gt;] [--force] [--recommended] [--tar &lt;tar&gt;] [--debug]

OPTIONS:
  --arch &lt;arch&gt;           The architecture of the kernel binary (values: amd64,
                          arm64) (default: arm64)
  --binary &lt;binary&gt;       Path to the kernel file (or archive member, if used
                          with --tar)
  --force                 Overwrites an existing kernel with the same name
  --recommended           Download and install the recommended kernel as the
                          default (takes precedence over all other flags)
  --tar &lt;tar&gt;             Filesystem path or remote URL to a tar archive
                          containing a kernel file
  --debug                 Enable debug output [environment: CONTAINER_DEBUG]
  --version               Show the version.
  -h, --help              Show help information.


```

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

`--recommended` 其实就是从 GitHub (kata-containers) 下载 kernel tar 包，国内/网络不好的话会非常慢。有两个更快的方案：

**方案 1：手动下载 tar 包后本地安装**

你可以用浏览器或其他加速工具（代理、镜像站等）先把 kernel tar 下载到本地，然后用本地路径安装：

```bash
# 先下载这个文件（约 120MB），可以用浏览器/代理/镜像加速
# https://github.com/kata-containers/kata-containers/releases/download/3.17.0/kata-static-3.17.0-arm64.tar.xz

# 下载完成后，用本地路径安装
container system kernel set --tar ~/Downloads/kata-static-3.17.0-arm64.tar.xz --binary opt/kata/share/kata-containers/vmlinux.container
```

**方案 2：用 curl 走代理下载**

如果你有代理，可以设置代理后用 curl 下载：

```bash
# 替换成你的代理地址
export https_proxy=http://127.0.0.1:7890

curl -L -o /tmp/kata-kernel.tar.xz \
  https://github.com/kata-containers/kata-containers/releases/download/3.17.0/kata-static-3.17.0-arm64.tar.xz

container system kernel set --tar /tmp/kata-kernel.tar.xz --binary opt/kata/share/kata-containers/vmlinux.container
```

你用哪种方式比较方便？如果你有代理的话，告诉我代理地址，我可以直接帮你下载安装。

[[Bug]: cannot get &#96;make&#96; to succeed in the &#96;kernel/&#96; folder · Issue #296 · apple/containerization](https://github.com/apple/containerization/issues/296)\n\n# Apple Container Kernel and Containerization Summary\n\nApple provides two main GitHub repositories for Linux containerization on macOS with Apple silicon:\n\n## Primary Repositories\n\n**apple/containerization** [1] is a Swift package that provides low-level APIs for running Linux containers on macOS. It includes:\n- OCI image management\n- ext4 filesystem creation\n- An optimized Linux kernel configuration for fast boot times\n- A lightweight init system called vminitd\n- Support for spawning virtual machines and containerized processes\n\n**apple/container** [2] is a command-line tool built on top of the Containerization package. It allows users to create and run OCI-compatible Linux containers as lightweight virtual machines on Mac with Apple silicon.\n\n## Kernel Configuration\n\nThe Containerization repository includes an optimized ARM64 Linux kernel configuration file (&#96;config-arm64&#96;) [3] based on Linux kernel version 6.1.68. This minimal kernel configuration is designed to support fast start times and lightweight container environments.\n\n## Known Issues\n\nUsers have reported issues with the &#96;system kernel set&#96; command, including problems with symlink handling in kernel tarballs and conflicts when setting alternative kernels [4]. Additionally, there have been build issues when compiling custom kernels in the &#96;kernel/&#96; directory [5].\n\nBoth projects are open-source under the Apache 2.0 license and actively maintained by Apple's development team.",

The reference covers the complete command-line interface, system management operations, XPC service protocols, configuration properties, and internal APIs.\nFor step-by-step usage guides and tutorials, see [Getting Started](https://deepwiki.com/apple/container/2-getting-started). For architectural details about how these components work internally, see [Architecture](https://deepwiki.com/apple/container/3-architecture).\n## Reference Architecture Overview\nThe container system\u0026#x27;s reference documentation is organized around the primary interfaces users interact with: the CLI commands, system management operations, and configuration system. The following diagram shows how user-facing commands map to their implementing code structures:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003c!--$!--\u003e\u003ctemplate data-dgst=\"BAILOUT_TO_CLIENT_SIDE_RENDERING\"\u003e\u003c/template\u003e\u003c!--/$--\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSources: [Sources/CLI/ContainerCLI.swift22-39](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/Sources/CLI/ContainerCLI.swift#L22-L39) [Sources/ContainerClient/Parser.swift42-671](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/Sources/ContainerClient/Parser.swift#L42-L671) [Sources/ContainerCommands/System/SystemStart.swift26-189](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/Sources/ContainerCommands/System/SystemStart.swift#L26-L189) [Sources/ContainerCommands/System/Property/PropertyList.swift23-96](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/Sources/ContainerCommands/System/Property/PropertyList.swift#L23-L96)\n## Command Parsing Architecture\nThe CLI system uses a structured approach to parse and validate user input. The &#96;Parser&#96; class provides static methods for converting string arguments into typed configuration objects:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003c!--$!--\u003e\u003ctemplate data-dgst=\"BAILOUT_TO_CLIENT_SIDE_RENDERING\"\u003e\u003c/template\u003e\u003c!--/$--\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSources: [Sources/ContainerClient/Parser.swift43-94](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/Sources/ContainerClient/Parser.swift#L43-L94) [Sources/ContainerClient/Parser.swift258-400](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/Sources/ContainerClient/Parser.swift#L258-L400) [Sources/ContainerClient/Parser.swift490-552](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/Sources/ContainerClient/Parser.swift#L490-L552)\n## System Configuration Architecture\nThe container system uses a centralized configuration store for managing system-wide properties. This diagram shows the relationship between property management commands and the underlying storage:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003c!--$!--\u003e\u003ctemplate data-dgst=\"BAILOUT_TO_CLIENT_SIDE_RENDERING\"\u003e\u003c/template\u003e\u003c!--/$--\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSources: [Sources/ContainerCommands/System/Property/PropertyList.swift23-96](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/Sources/ContainerCommands/System/Property/PropertyList.swift#L23-L96) [Sources/ContainerCommands/System/Property/PropertySet.swift26-80](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/Sources/ContainerCommands/System/Property/PropertySet.swift#L26-L80) [Sources/ContainerCommands/System/Property/PropertyGet.swift24-53](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/Sources/ContainerCommands/System/Property/PropertyGet.swift#L24-L53) [Sources/ContainerCommands/System/Property/PropertyClear.swift24-46](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/Sources/ContainerCommands/System/Property/PropertyClear.swift#L24-L46)\n## Reference Organization\nThe reference documentation is organized into the following sections:\n|Section|Purpose|Key Components|\n|[CLI Commands](https://deepwiki.com/apple/container/5.1-building-from-source)|Complete command reference|&#96;container run&#96;, &#96;container build&#96;, image/network/volume management|\n|[System Management](https://deepwiki.com/apple/container/5.2-dependencies)|System-level operations|Service lifecycle, DNS, kernel, properties|\n|[XPC Services](https://deepwiki.com/apple/container/5.3-testing)|Inter-process communication|Service interfaces, message formats|\n|[Configuration](https://deepwiki.com/apple/container/5.4-cicd-pipeline)|System configuration|Properties, environment variables, defaults|\n|[Image Management](https://deepwiki.com/apple/container/5.5-contributing)|Image service APIs|Content store, lifecycle management|\n|[Builder Components](#5.6)|Build system reference|Protocol buffers, builder integration|\n## Command Categories\nThe CLI provides commands organized into logical groups:\n|Category|Commands|Implementation|\n|**Container Lifecycle**|&#96;run&#96;, &#96;create&#96;, &#96;start&#96;, &#96;stop&#96;, &#96;kill&#96;, &#96;delete&#96;|&#96;Application.Run&#96;, &#96;Application.Create&#96;, etc.|\n|**Image Management**|&#96;image list&#96;, &#96;image pull&#96;, &#96;image push&#96;, &#96;image save&#96;, &#96;image load&#96;|Image service XPC calls|\n|**Builder Operations**|&#96;build&#96;, &#96;builder start&#96;, &#96;builder stop&#96;, &#96;builder status&#96;|BuildKit integration via gRPC|\n|**Network Management**|&#96;network create&#96;, &#96;network list&#96;, &#96;network delete&#96;, &#96;network inspect&#96;|Network service XPC calls|\n|**Volume Management**|&#96;volume create&#96;, &#96;volume list&#96;, &#96;volume delete&#96;, &#96;volume inspect&#96;|Volume service XPC calls|\n|**System Administration**|&#96;system start&#96;, &#96;system stop&#96;, &#96;system status&#96;, &#96;system logs&#96;|Service management, logging|\n|**Configuration**|&#96;system property list/get/set/clear&#96;|&#96;DefaultsStore&#96; operations|\n|**DNS Management**|&#96;system dns create/delete/list&#96;|&#96;HostDNSResolver&#96; operations|\n|**Kernel Management**|&#96;system kernel set&#96;|Kernel installation and configuration|\nSources: [docs/command-reference.md1-913](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/docs/command-reference.md?plain=1#L1-L913) [Sources/ContainerCommands/System/SystemStart.swift26-189](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/Sources/ContainerCommands/System/SystemStart.swift#L26-L189)\n## Validation and Error Handling\nThe system provides comprehensive input validation through the &#96;Parser&#96; class and specialized validators:\n|Input Type|Validation Method|Error Handling|\n|**Memory Specifications**|&#96;Parser.memoryString()&#96;|&#96;ContainerizationError.invalidArgument&#96;|\n|**Mount Specifications**|&#96;Parser.mount()&#96;, &#96;Parser.validateMount()&#96;|Path existence, directory validation|\n|**Port Specifications**|&#96;Parser.publishPort()&#96;|Protocol, address, port range validation|\n|**Domain Names**|&#96;Parser.isValidDomainName()&#96;|RFC-compliant domain syntax|\n|**Volume Names**|&#96;VolumeStorage.isValidVolumeName()&#96;|Pattern matching validation|\n|**User Specifications**|&#96;Parser.user()&#96;|UID/GID parsing and validation|\n|**Platform Specifications**|&#96;Parser.platform()&#96;|OS/architecture validation|\nSources: [Sources/ContainerClient/Parser.swift43-94](https://github.com/apple/container/blob/0760791a/Sources/ContainerClient/Parser.swift#L43-L94) = nil\n\n @Flag(name: .long, help: \"Overwrites an existing kernel with the same name\")\n var force: Bool = false\n\n @Flag(name: .long, help: \"Download and install the recommended kernel as the default (takes precedence over all other flags)\")\n var recommended: Bool = false\n\n @Option(name: .customLong(\"tar\"), help: \"Filesystem path or remote URL to a tar archive containing a kernel file\")\n var tarPath: String? = nil\n\n @OptionGroup\n public var logOptions: Flags.Logging\n\n public init() {}\n\n public func run() async throws {\n if recommended {\n let url = DefaultsStore.get(key: .defaultKernelURL)\n let path = DefaultsStore.get(key: .defaultKernelBinaryPath)\n print(\"Installing the recommended kernel from \\(url)...\")\n try await Self.downloadAndInstallWithProgressBar(tarRemoteURL: url, kernelFilePath: path, force: force)\n return\n }\n guard tarPath != nil else {\n return try await self.setKernelFromBinary()\n }\n try await self.setKernelFromTar()\n }\n\n private func setKernelFromBinary() async throws {\n guard let binaryPath else {\n throw ArgumentParser.ValidationError(\"missing argument '--binary'\")\n }\n let absolutePath = URL(fileURLWithPath: binaryPath, relativeTo: .currentDirectory()).absoluteURL.absoluteString\n let platform = try getSystemPlatform()\n try await ClientKernel.installKernel(kernelFilePath: absolutePath, platform: platform, force: force)\n }\n\n private func setKernelFromTar() async throws {\n guard let binaryPath else {\n throw ArgumentParser.ValidationError(\"missing argument '--binary'\")\n }\n guard let tarPath else {\n throw ArgumentParser.ValidationError(\"missing argument '--tar\")\n }\n let platform = try getSystemPlatform()\n let localTarPath = URL(fileURLWithPath: tarPath, relativeTo: .currentDirectory()).path\n let fm = FileManager.default\n if fm.fileExists(atPath: localTarPath) {\n try await ClientKernel.installKernelFromTar(tarFile: localTarPath, kernelFilePath: binaryPath, platform: platform, force: force)\n return\n }\n guard let remoteURL = URL(string: tarPath) else {\n throw ContainerizationError(.invalidArgument, message: \"invalid remote URL '\\(tarPath)' for argument '--tar'. Missing protocol?\")\n }\n try await Self.downloadAndInstallWithProgressBar(tarRemoteURL: remoteURL.absoluteString, kernelFilePath: binaryPath, platform: platform, force: force)\n }\n\n private func getSystemPlatform() throws -\u003e SystemPlatform {\n switch arch {\n case \"arm64\":\n return .linuxArm\n case \"amd64\":\n return .linuxAmd\n default:\n throw ContainerizationError(.unsupported, message: \"unsupported architecture \\(arch)\")\n }\n }\n\n static func downloadAndInstallWithProgressBar(tarRemoteURL: String, kernelFilePath: String, platform: SystemPlatform = .current, force: Bool) async throws {\n let progressConfig = try ProgressConfig(\n showTasks: true,\n totalTasks: 2\n )\n let progress = ProgressBar(config: progressConfig)\n defer {\n progress.finish()\n }\n progress.start()\n try await ClientKernel.installKernelFromTar(tarFile: tarRemoteURL, kernelFilePath: kernelFilePath, platform: platform, progressUpdate: progress.handler, force: force)\n progress.finish()\n }\n\n }\n}\n",

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

好，两个镜像总计：
- **vminit**: ~60MB (1 个 layer)
- **builder**: ~106MB (7 个 layer)

总共约 166MB。`ghcr.io` 的 token 不需要认证就能拿到，也就是说 blob 可以直接通过 URL 下载。我用 ghfast 镜像加速把这些 blob 手动下载下来，然后用 `container image load` 导入。

不过更简单的方案是：**直接用 skopeo 或者 crane 把镜像下载成 OCI tar 包，再 load 进去**。但你可能没装这些工具。

让我试最直接的方案——给 `container image pull` 配置一个 registry mirror：

---

_**User**_

针对国内下载 ghcr.io 镜像缓慢问题，核心解决方法是使用国内镜像代理或自建代理。最直接的方法是使用南京大学等高校镜像站（如将 ghcr.io 替换为 ghcr.nju.edu.cn），或通过 DockerHub 国内镜像缓存项目加速。 
GitHub
GitHub
 +1
具体加速方案如下：
1. 使用国内代理镜像站 (推荐)：
直接修改镜像地址，在 ghcr.io 前面加上镜像站前缀。
南京大学： ghcr.nju.edu.cn
示例： docker pull ghcr.io/linuxserver/webtop -> docker pull ghcr.nju.edu.cn/linuxserver/webtop

---

builder 的 index 里有个 attestation manifest 也被引用了但我们没下载它。让我修复一下，只保留 arm64 的 manifest 在 index 里。

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /tmp/oci-builder && python3 -c "
import json, hashlib

# Read current index
with open('index.json') as f:
    idx = json.load(f)

# Get the index manifest digest from the current index.json
old_digest = idx['manifests'][0]['digest']
old_hash = old_digest.split(':')[1]

# Read the original index manifest
with open(f'blobs/sha256/{old_hash}') as f:
    orig = json.load(f)

# Filter to only arm64 linux manifest
filtered = [m for m in orig['manifests'] 
            if m.get('platform',{}).get('architecture') == 'arm64' 
            and m.get('platform',{}).get('os') == 'linux']

orig['manifests'] = filtered
new_content = json.dumps(orig)
new_hash = hashlib.sha256(new_content.encode()).hexdigest()

# Write new index manifest
with open(f'blobs/sha256/{new_hash}', 'w') as f:
    f.write(new_content)

# Update index.json
idx['manifests'][0]['digest'] = f'sha256:{new_hash}'
idx['manifests'][0]['size'] = len(new_content)
with open('index.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(idx, f)

print(f'Fixed: removed attestation manifest, new hash: {new_hash[:12]}')
" 2>&1</summary>

```bash
cd /tmp/oci-builder && python3 -c "
import json, hashlib

# Read current index
with open('index.json') as f:
    idx = json.load(f)

# Get the index manifest digest from the current index.json
old_digest = idx['manifests'][0]['digest']
old_hash = old_digest.split(':')[1]

# Read the original index manifest
with open(f'blobs/sha256/{old_hash}') as f:
    orig = json.load(f)

# Filter to only arm64 linux manifest
filtered = [m for m in orig['manifests'] 
            if m.get('platform',{}).get('architecture') == 'arm64' 
            and m.get('platform',{}).get('os') == 'linux']

orig['manifests'] = filtered
new_content = json.dumps(orig)
new_hash = hashlib.sha256(new_content.encode()).hexdigest()

# Write new index manifest
with open(f'blobs/sha256/{new_hash}', 'w') as f:
    f.write(new_content)

# Update index.json
idx['manifests'][0]['digest'] = f'sha256:{new_hash}'
idx['manifests'][0]['size'] = len(new_content)
with open('index.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(idx, f)

print(f'Fixed: removed attestation manifest, new hash: {new_hash[:12]}')
" 2>&1
```

```
Fixed: removed attestation manifest, new hash: b02bc03bd1e1

```
</details>
</tool-use>

OCI layout 的 `index.json` 需要加 `annotations` 来指定 tag。让我修复两个镜像的 index.json，加上 `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name` annotation。

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 -c "
import json

# Fix vminit
with open('/tmp/oci-vminit/index.json') as f:
    idx = json.load(f)
idx['manifests'][0]['annotations'] = {'org.opencontainers.image.ref.name': 'ghcr.io/apple/containerization/vminit:0.26.3'}
with open('/tmp/oci-vminit/index.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(idx, f)
print('vminit index.json fixed')

# Fix builder
with open('/tmp/oci-builder/index.json') as f:
    idx = json.load(f)
idx['manifests'][0]['annotations'] = {'org.opencontainers.image.ref.name': 'ghcr.io/apple/container-builder-shim/builder:0.8.0'}
with open('/tmp/oci-builder/index.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(idx, f)
print('builder index.json fixed')
" 2>&1</summary>

好消息！builder 已经在运行了，`node:22-slim` 镜像也已经拉下来了。现在的情况比之前好很多。

构建命令就是 `container build -t nanoclaw-agent:latest .`，在 `container/` 目录下执行。Dockerfile 需要从 Debian 源安装 chromium 等包，再从 npm 安装全局包。主要的网络瓶颈是：

1. `apt-get update && apt-get install` — Debian 源
2. `npm install -g agent-browser @anthropic-ai/claude-code` — npm 源
3. `npm install` — 内部依赖

让我先准备一个用国内镜像加速的 Dockerfile，然后构建。

找到问题了！**IP forwarding 是关闭的** (`net.inet.ip.forwarding: 0`)，而且 `bridge100`（vmnet 的桥接接口）没有 IPv4 地址。这就是容器无法访问外网的根本原因。

Apple Container 的 vmnet 网络需要 macOS 的 Internet Sharing（互联网共享）来提供 NAT。让我尝试启用。

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

还是不行。这是 Apple Container 的 vmnet NAT 根本没工作。这是个已知问题——macOS 26 上 Apple Container 的 vmnet 需要额外的系统权限或配置来启用 NAT。

既然容器内没有外网，那我换一个策略：**在宿主机上预先下载所有需要的东西，用 bind mount 传入容器来构建**。或者更实际的方案——**直接用多阶段构建，在宿主机上安装依赖后打包进镜像**。

不过最简单的方案是——检查是否有端口发布（port-publish）方式能绕过，或者先用 `container build` 的 `--network` 参数。

构建日志显示 `apt-get update` 时 `Ign:1 http://mirrors.ustc.edu.cn/debian bookworm InRelease` —— 这说明 DNS 能解析但 HTTP 连不上。和 `container run` 的情况一样。

核心问题就是 **Apple Container 的 vmnet NAT 不工作**。让我来修复这个问题。

[bridge100 using 192.168.105.1/24 subnet which breaks container networking · Issue #109 · apple/container](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/109)\n\n# Apple Container vmnet NAT Internet Access Issues and Workarounds\n\n## Primary Issue: No Internet Access During Container Build\n\nThe main reported problem is that containers cannot access external internet resources during the build process, even though they can fetch OCI images.[1] Specifically:\n\n- **Cargo/Rust builds fail** when trying to download dependencies from crates.io with timeout errors[1]\n- **DNS resolution works** for some domains but connection timeouts occur for package repositories[1]\n- The issue appears to be NAT/network routing related despite basic DNS functioning[1]\n\n## Known Issues (as of 2026)\n\n**Build-time internet access:** Issue #656 remains open as of March 2026, with users unable to access external URLs (like crates.io) during container builds despite successful OCI image pulls.[1]\n\n**Host service access:** This was temporarily disabled prior to commit &#96;1942a39&#96; due to CI issues with missing &#96;/etc/resolver&#96; directories, but was expected to be fixed in a release scheduled for late February 2026.[2]\n\n**Advanced routing limitations:** Containers cannot route through custom host network interfaces like Cloudflare Zero Trust WARP (utun devices), limiting access to VPN-provided networks even when the host can access them.[3]\n\n## Current Workarounds\n\nThe search results do not provide explicit workarounds for the NAT/internet access issue during builds. The issues remain open, suggesting the community is still awaiting official fixes rather than documented workarounds for this specific problem.",
      "title": "Web search results for query: apple container vmnet NAT no internet access build \"container build\" workaround 2026"
    }

I'm surprised no one has addressed this, it really limits image builds.\n\n**@ulf-engels** commented · Nov 5, 2025 at 7am\n\n\u003e Hi,\n\u003e \n\u003e I can confirm that networking (especially name resolution) is quite limited in cnotainer yet. But I found a workaround for \"container run\". Her an example\n\u003e \n\u003e If I run an alpine container \"the normal way\" I am unable to fetch package updates\n\u003e \n\u003e &#96;container run -it alpine\n\u003e / # apk update\n\u003e fetch https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.22/main/aarch64/APKINDEX.tar.gz\n\u003e WARNING: updating and opening https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.22/main: temporary error (try again later)\n\u003e fetch https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.22/community/aarch64/APKINDEX.tar.gz\n\u003e WARNING: updating and opening https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.22/community: temporary error (try again later)&#96;\n\u003e \n\u003e But if I add the \"--dns\" parameter, it works nicely (you can change the IP after \"dns\" to any other reachable DNS of your choice)\n\u003e \n\u003e &#96;container run -it --dns 8.8.8.8 alpine\n\u003e / # apk update\n\u003e fetch https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.22/main/aarch64/APKINDEX.tar.gz\n\u003e fetch https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.22/community/aarch64/APKINDEX.tar.gz\n\u003e v3.22.2-114-g3bc4c29c93b [https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.22/main]\n\u003e v3.22.2-131-g6a96199c31d [https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.22/community]\n\u003e OK: 26161 distinct packages available\n\u003e / # apk add curl\n\u003e (1/9) Installing brotli-libs (1.1.0-r2)\n\u003e (2/9) Installing c-ares (1.34.5-r0)\n\u003e (3/9) Installing libunistring (1.3-r0)\n\u003e (4/9) Installing libidn2 (2.3.7-r0)\n\u003e (5/9) Installing nghttp2-libs (1.65.0-r0)\n\u003e (6/9) Installing libpsl (0.21.5-r3)\n\u003e (7/9) Installing zstd-libs (1.5.7-r0)\n\u003e (8/9) Installing libcurl (8.14.1-r2)\n\u003e (9/9) Installing curl (8.14.1-r2)\n\u003e Executing busybox-1.37.0-r19.trigger\n\u003e OK: 13 MiB in 25 packages\n\u003e / #\n\u003e &#96;\n\u003e \n\u003e Unfortunately I did not find a way (yet) to set the DNS for the buildkit container. Hints or the \"--dns\" option for the build command would be highly appreciated\n\n**@theZMC** commented · Nov 7, 2025 at 3:57am\n\n\u003e A temporary workaround I've found is to &#96;exec&#96; into the &#96;buildkit&#96; container and modify the &#96;/etc/resolv.conf&#96; to point to a known good DNS server:\n\u003e \n\u003e &#96;&#96;&#96;sh\n\u003e container exec buildkit /bin/sh -c 'echo \"nameserver 8.8.8.8\" \u003e /etc/resolv.conf'\n\u003e &#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003e \n\u003e Obviously not an ideal long-term solution but should unblock in the short term.\n\n**@saccy** commented · Nov 7, 2025 at 5:58am\n\n\u003e Same train of though as @theZMC - Just modified &#96;/etc/resolver/containerization.container&#96; (this is on your mac filesystem) by adding google dns followed by a reboot, seems to work although download speeds during build process are all over the place for me.\n\u003e \n\u003e &#96;/etc/resolver/containerization.container&#96;:\n\u003e \n\u003e &#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003e domain container\n\u003e search container\n\u003e nameserver 8.8.8.8 127.0.0.1\n\u003e port 2053\n\u003e &#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003e \n\u003e (Running behind cloudflare doesn't seem to work at all)\n\n**@jiridanek** commented · Nov 22, 2025 at 8:25pm\n\n\u003e If you're running a local DNS resolver, the cause may be\n\u003e \n\u003e - https://github.com/apple/container/issues/402\n\n**andybons** mentioned this in PR [#45: refactor(id): unify ID format and add Apple container build support](https://github.com/majorcontext/moat/pull/45) · Jan 21, 2026 at 7:21pm\n\n**claude[bot]** mentioned this in PR [#75: fix(apple): simplify builder DNS configuration](https://github.com/majorcontext/moat/pull/75) · Jan 28, 2026 at 7:34pm\n\n**@jongross** commented · Feb 1, 2026 at 7:31pm\n\n\u003e I just ran into this issue as well, and would love to see this fixed!\n\n**@jmasson** commented · Feb 5, 2026 at 10:57am\n\n\u003e Oddly I had no issues on 0.8 but after updating to 0.9 and trying to build the same image, I hit this issue ... worked around it by running &#96;container exec buildkit /bin/sh -c 'echo \"nameserver 8.8.8.8\" \u003e /etc/resolv.conf'&#96; after starting the container service.\n\n**@iamrecursion** commented · Mar 5, 2026 at 2:38pm\n\n\u003e Been running into this one as well and did the same edit to &#96;/etc/resolv.conf&#96; in the &#96;buildkit&#96; builder.",

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

从宿主机也 ping 不通 buildkit 容器！vmnet 网络完全不工作。Apple Container 在你的 macOS 26.3 上网络配置有严重问题。

这是一个已知的 Apple Container bug。根据之前的 issue 讨论，核心问题是 IP forwarding 没有启用。**你需要在终端里手动执行一条 sudo 命令来修复**：

```bash
sudo sysctl -w net.inet.ip.forwarding=1
```

请你在系统终端中执行这条命令（需要输入密码），完成后告诉我，我再继续构建。

_**User**_

https://github.com/apple/container  这里面有解决方案吗

---

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

根据我之前查到的 [issue #656](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/656)，这是一个已知的 Apple Container bug——**构建时容器内没有外网访问**。目前社区给出了几个 workaround：

1. **修改 buildkit 容器的 DNS**（最多人用的方案）：
   ```bash
   container exec buildkit /bin/sh -c 'echo "nameserver 8.8.8.8" > /etc/resolv.conf'
   ```

2. **修改宿主机的 resolver 文件**（另一个方案）

3. **启用 IP forwarding**（根据 [issue #989](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/989)）：
   ```bash
   sudo sysctl -w net.inet.ip.forwarding=1
   ```

但我们实测发现，**你的环境中不只是 DNS 问题，是 VM 完全没有外网连通性**——连宿主机都 ping 不通 buildkit 容器的 IP。所以上面方案 1 和 2 只能修 DNS，修不了底层网络。

**真正需要的是方案 3：启用 IP forwarding。** 这需要 sudo 权限，我在 Cursor 终端里无法执行。

请你在系统终端里执行以下命令：

```bash
sudo sysctl -w net.inet.ip.forwarding=1
```

执行完后告诉我，我立刻验证网络并继续构建。这条命令重启后会失效，如果想永久生效可以后续再配置。

---

_**User**_

执行完了

---

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

好，让我立刻验证网络并开始构建。
